philosophy.network_soil: The Soil (Network/Graph) represents objective facts, laws, and reality constraints.
philosophy.tree_fate: The Tree (Fate) represents user background and decision choices growing out of the soil.
philosophy.core_claim: The Soil provides objective facts/resistance; the Tree presents personal choices and possibilities, ensuring controllable Plan B side buds.

smart_pruning_policy.rationale: Prevents decision paralysis by highlighting Top-3 optimal paths and collapsing extreme edge cases into expandable drawers.

dual_track_interaction.nl_to_gui_sync: Natural language commands (e.g., 'Change my German level to B1') automatically trigger UI parameter slider highlights and re-render tree state.
dual_track_interaction.gui_to_nl_sync: Dragging sliders or toggling nodes in GUI automatically updates underlying user profile state and recalculates graph pathways.

TRUNK.description: User baseline current state, local memory, and validated status.

MAIN_BRANCH.description: Major decision pathways. Top-3 visible by default.

SIDE_BUD.description: Hypothetical what-if variables (e.g. language score, salary bump).

DEAD_BRANCH.description: Blocked or high-risk decision pathways. Collapsed by default.

PLAN_B_HEDGE.description: Alternative risk-hedging pathway rendered as an auxiliary root/branch.
